posts.name: Don Cornutt
posts.cooked:
1999 Rene Engel Vosne Romanee Brulees

Part of a pre-dinner tasting for the Tastevin. A gorgeous red fruited nose. Crunchy fruit. Lively. Red. So pure. Coated with rose petals and spice.
The palate is precise and shows perfectly ripe red fruit with spice. Some element of forest floor with time. Very long finish. Not roasted at all. So satisfying. This is special. Wow!

At our dinner we are also having the 1999 Grands Echezeaux. Philippe really put out great wines in 1999. RIP.

posts.name: john stimson
posts.cooked: The GE was very nice a year or two ago courtesy of a friend. It’s rather sad when good estates move. I’ve lost track. Is it vougerie now?

posts.name: CHIU LIN

My two cases of '96 Engel (Clos Vougeots and Echezeaux) are not doing too well though, maybe they did better in '99, but '96 certainly disappointing.

posts.name: suzanne_camhi
posts.cooked:
john stimson:

The GE was very nice a year or two ago courtesy of a friend. It’s rather sad when good estates move. I’ve lost track. Is it vougerie now?

it is Domain D’ Eugenie now starting in 2006

domaine-eugenie.com

Domaine d'Eugénie

Le Domaine d'Eugénie est né du rachat du domaine René Engel par François Pinault en juillet 2006. Il est situé à Vosne-Romanée, au cœur de la Côte-de-Nuits entre Nuits-Saint-Georges et Gevrey-Chambertin

posts.name: Phil Smith
posts.cooked: Brings back memories. Sometime around '05 I was living in Columbus Ohio where there was a distributer close-out that included '99 Engel GE at some silly-low price (around $25/bottle IIRC). I got a half case and long since drank through and enjoyed them. Got an email offer last week on the same wine for something like $700/bottle. Wish I would have kept a few…

posts.name: Don Cornutt
posts.cooked: Note on the Grands Echezeaux coming soon!

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
A strong case could be made that 1999 was Engel’s strongest vintage. For me the other contenders would be 1993 and 2002, which are also really successful, but I think just a bit more marked by the vintages as opposed to the sites. Whereas the 1999s are lovely glasses of Pinot and very clearly differentiated as Clos Vougeot, Grands Echezeaux etc.

The 1996s by contrast I have had very bad luck with. A long-time Engel importer in the UK told me the domaine suffered with very poor corks that year. I have had corked bottles, volatile bottles and bottles that are just not very compelling, with the ‘rusty pipe’ aspect that some commentators have identified as a characteristic of the vintage’s failures. Of course, I wouldn’t turn down the chance to try one given the domaine is no longer extant, but the 1996 Engels are not the bottles I would grab in the cellar if I wanted to convince someone of the pleasures Burgundy can offer!

PCLIN:

Eugenie now.

My two cases of '96 Engel (Clos Vougeots and Echezeaux) are not doing too well though, maybe they did better in '99, but '96 certainly disappointing.

My 96 GEs have really been pale imitations of the 98-03 renditions…

posts.name: Stuart BeauneHead Niemtzow
posts.cooked:
FWIW, the last of several visits I made to that estate from 1988 was in 2001 (Philippe died in 2005). My memory was that I was unimpressed with what I tasted in barrel, but my notes “refreshed my recollection” that though the 2000s were so so…the '99s were consistently terrific.

My last memory of Philippe is from March 2001. He was sitting in his cuverie playing cards with some friends with a bottle of marc de bourgogne on the table. His brother showed us around that trip…as Philippe was intensly involved in his “jeux”.

A gentle, but troubled soul…and a big loss when the domaine, as such, left the wine world following his sudden death.

The 2000 GE is interstellar Stuart, at least compared to the 96s that heve been referenced. I believe the 99 will be better, but it isn’t currently - or at least it wasn’t just over a year ago - but that’s 99 for you…

posts.name: Stuart BeauneHead Niemtzow
posts.cooked:
Bill…my notes from that day reflect that I felt that the CV and the GE were terrific in both vintages. The dropoff was the other wines.

I long felt that their CV was as good as that vineyard gets. Their GE was, for me, one of the very top wines in all Burgundy when Philippe made it…and maybe before. So, i have no doubt that it was/is “interstellar”…it always is/was…no matter the vintage.

When first visited, in 1988, it was because I had tasted the '85 CV at a tasting here that spring. So, I wrote the estate and asked to visit. When I got there, I then realized that GE was the real giant of the stable…and one of Burgundy’s best wines. I remember being surprised, as I loved their CV, and that’s what they were known for due to Philippe’s grandfather, Rene (who was present that day) being a big Tastevin guy. Unfortunately, I only have 4-5 bottles in total of their GE left in my cellar…and am looking forward to trying the '88 in two years…and maybe an '86 later this year…
